response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and excellent communication skills, this is a poor fit for the role. The position requires 5–10 years of professional C++ development on Windows with mandatory capital markets or fixed income domain expertise. The resume shows only ~6 months of recent C++ work (National Instruments, 2014–2015), no Windows platform development experience, and zero capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking background. The candidate's 15+ year career is predominantly in web development, mobile apps, and data integration systems—fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements.
strengths: Demonstrated expertise in high-performance, low-latency systems (Dunnhumby handled millions of HTTP requests/second using asynchronous I/O and caching strategies)
Strong software engineering practices including CI/CD pipeline knowledge, infrastructure setup, TDD, and version control expertise
Excellent communication and mentoring abilities, evidenced by workshops, conference talks, and sustained open-source project maintenance
concerns: Only ~6 months of documented C++ professional experience (National Instruments, 2014–2015) falls drastically short of the mandatory 5–10 years requirement; C++ absent from recent skills
No capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking domain experience—a mandatory qualification for this consulting engagement
No evidence of Windows platform development or SQL Server/Oracle proficiency; career focused on web/Linux environments with primarily TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise gap (6 months vs. 5-10 years required)
Missing capital markets/fixed income domain experience
Strong software craftsmanship and infrastructure knowledge
